methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: The Rollup contract must hold enough stake tokens to cover all active stakes and withdrawable funds.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the contract balance is less than the total obligations, users or validators will not be able to withdraw their funds, leading to insolvency and loss of funds.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_rollup_solvency() public view { assert(IERC20(stakeToken).balanceOf(address(this)) >= totalWithdrawableFunds + sumAllStakes()); }

invariants.description: The EdgeChallengeManager must hold enough stake tokens to refund the winner of every active challenge.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the contract balance is less than the required refunds, the winning validator of a challenge cannot be refunded, breaking the economic incentive to challenge invalid states.

invariants.description: The delay buffer blocks must always remain within the defined bounds (between threshold and max).
invariants.attack_scenario: If the buffer drops below the threshold or exceeds the max, the sequencer could be unfairly penalized or given too much leeway, breaking the force-inclusion timing guarantees.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_buffer_bounds() public view { assert(buffer.bufferBlocks >= buffer.threshold && buffer.bufferBlocks <= buffer.max); }

invariants.description: The totalWithdrawableFunds accumulator must always equal the sum of all individual withdrawable funds.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the accumulator diverges, it indicates a flaw in the accounting logic which could be exploited to trap funds or mint phantom withdrawable balances.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_total_withdrawable_funds() public view { assert(totalWithdrawableFunds == sumAllWithdrawableFunds()); }

invariants.description: A user's deposit balance can only decrease via withdrawal, and the total deposit balances must reflect the pool's liabilities.
invariants.attack_scenario: If a user can artificially inflate their depositBalance without transferring tokens, they could drain the pool when the stake is returned, stealing from other depositors.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_pool_liabilities() public view { assert(sumDepositBalances() == totalDeposited - totalWithdrawn); }
